menu.name: White Bean Arugula Salad with Cherry Tomatoes and Balsamic
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white beans (from 3/4 cup dried cannellini beans)
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Assemble arugula, cooked white beans, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and basil in a large bowl
Whisk together dressing ingredients and toss with salad
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately
agent_notes: Simple assembly with weekend-prepped beans and pre-made dressing

menu.name: Spiced Chickpea and Spinach Salad with Cumin-Lime Dressing
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chickpeas (from 3/4 cup dried chickpeas)
night_of_steps_list: Toss cooked chickpeas with 1 tbsp olive oil, cumin, and paprika; roast at 400°F for 15 minutes until crispy
Combine spinach, cucumber, bell pepper, red onion, and cilantro in a large bowl
Add warm chickpeas, drizzle with prepared dressing, and toss to combine
agent_notes: Chickpeas get a quick roast for texture; most components prepped on weekend

menu.name: Black Bean and Avocado Salad with Cilantro-Lime Vinaigrette
ingredients: 2 cups cooked black beans (from 3/4 cup dried black beans)
night_of_steps_list: Chop romaine lettuce and place in large serving bowl
Add cooked black beans, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cilantro
Dice avocado fresh, add to salad, drizzle with prepared dressing and toss gently
agent_notes: Avocado diced fresh to prevent browning; everything else prepped ahead

menu.name: Buffalo Chickpea Salad with Creamy Ranch
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chickpeas (from 3/4 cup dried chickpeas)
night_of_steps_list: Toss cooked chickpeas with buffalo sauce and olive oil; roast at 400°F for 15-20 minutes
Combine mixed greens, carrots, celery, and red onion in a large bowl
Top with buffalo chickpeas and drizzle with prepared ranch dressing
agent_notes: Fun flavor twist with buffalo sauce; chickpeas roasted fresh for best texture

menu.name: Lentil and Roasted Vegetable Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing
ingredients: 2 cups cooked green or brown lentils (from 3/4 cup dried lentils)
1 red bell pepper, cut into chunks
1 yellow bell pepper, cut into chunks
1 small red onion, cut into wedges
night_of_steps_list: Toss cherry tomatoes, bell peppers, and red onion with olive oil, salt, and pepper; roast at 425°F for 25 minutes
Arrange mixed greens on plates, top with cooked lentils and roasted vegetables
Drizzle with prepared lemon-tahini dressing and garnish with fresh parsley
agent_notes: Vegetables roasted fresh for optimal flavor; lentils and dressing prepped ahead

menu.name: Indian-Spiced Kidney Bean and Spinach Salad with Mint-Yogurt Dressing
ingredients: 2 cups cooked kidney beans (from 3/4 cup dried kidney beans)
night_of_steps_list: Heat olive oil and cumin seeds until fragrant; toss with cooked kidney beans, coriander, and turmeric
Combine spinach, cucumber, tomato, red onion, and green chili in a large bowl
Add spiced kidney beans, drizzle with prepared mint-yogurt dressing, and toss to combine
agent_notes: Quick spice blooming adds warmth; kidney beans cooked ahead for convenience

menu.name: Marinated Tofu and Arugula Salad with Ginger-Miso Dressing
ingredients: 14 oz extra-firm tofu, pressed and cubed
night_of_steps_list: Marinate pressed tofu cubes in soy sauce, rice vinegar, maple syrup, and sesame oil for 15 minutes
Pan-fry marinated tofu in olive oil over medium-high heat until golden and crispy, about 10 minutes
Combine arugula, red cabbage, carrots, and green onions; top with crispy tofu, drizzle with prepared ginger-miso dressing, and garnish with sesame seeds
agent_notes: Tofu marinated and cooked fresh for best texture; vegetables and dressing prepped ahead

saturday: Soak chickpeas (1.5 cups dried) in cold water, 8pm Saturday for 12 hours
Soak white beans (3/4 cup dried) in cold water, 8pm Saturday for 12 hours
Soak black beans (3/4 cup dried) in cold water, 8pm Saturday for 12 hours
sunday: Cook soaked chickpeas: drain, add fresh water, simmer 60-90 minutes until tender; yield 3.75 cups (use for Tuesday & Thursday)
Cook soaked white beans: drain, add fresh water, simmer 60-75 minutes until tender; yield 2 cups (use for Monday)
Cook soaked black beans: drain, add fresh water, simmer 60-90 minutes until tender; yield 2 cups (use for Wednesday)
Cook lentils (3/4 cup dried): rinse, simmer in 2 cups water for 20-25 minutes until tender; yield 2 cups (use for Friday)
Cook kidney beans (3/4 cup dried): rinse, boil for 10 minutes, then simmer 60-75 minutes until tender; yield 2 cups (use for Saturday)
Wash and dry all greens: arugula, spinach, mixed greens, romaine; store in containers with paper towels
Chop vegetables: halve cherry tomatoes (4.5 cups total), dice cucumbers (2 total), dice bell peppers (3 total), slice red onions (1.5 total)
Shred carrots (2 cups) and red cabbage (1 cup)
Press tofu: wrap 14 oz extra-firm tofu in towels, press with weight for 30 minutes, cube and refrigerate
Make Monday's dressing: whisk balsamic vinegar, olive oil, garlic, Dijon, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Tuesday's dressing: whisk lime juice, olive oil, cumin, honey/maple, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Wednesday's dressing: whisk lime juice, olive oil, garlic, cumin, cilantro, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Thursday's ranch dressing: mix Greek yogurt, mayo, lemon juice, garlic, dill, chives, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Friday's tahini dressing: whisk tahini, lemon juice, water, garlic, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Saturday's mint-yogurt dressing: mix Greek yogurt, mint, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper; store in jar
Make Sunday's ginger-miso dressing: whisk miso, rice vinegar, ginger, sesame oil, maple syrup, water; store in jar
Chop fresh herbs: basil (1/4 cup), cilantro (1/2 cup), parsley (1/4 cup), mint (2 tbsp), dill (2 tbsp), chives (1 tbsp); store separately

bean_schedule.cook: Sunday morning, drain and simmer 60-90 minutes
Sunday morning, drain and simmer 60-75 minutes
Sunday morning, drain and simmer 60-90 minutes

bean_schedule.soak: none (but recommended for 4-8 hours to reduce cooking time)
bean_schedule.cook: Sunday, boil hard for 10 minutes, then simmer 60-75 minutes

produce: Bell peppers: 1 red, 2 yellow, 1 additional for roasting
dried_beans_legumes: Dried green or brown lentils (3/4 cup)
